ONCOSYS Orientation (updated on 20.8.202 1) 
 
Most of the following information can be found in the intra net pages either from Flamma or Biomedicum 
intra , please learn how to use them : 
University intranet pages : Flamma https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/flamma/ 
Biomedicum intranet pages : https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/ if you haven’t registered yet, please do 
so first: Go to the following page, and identify yourself using your Office 365 account: 
https://accessrequestform.azurewebsites.net/ 
 
Apply for the Universit y user name: https://helpdesk.it.helsinki.fi/en/instructions/logging -and-
connections/user -account/instruc tions -completing -username -application -form

and-connections/user -account/validity -period -access -rights -and-its-extension 
 
Keys https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Keys, -Security -Surveillance, -Identification -Card.aspx 
ONCOSYS entrance room B320a is open Mon -Fri from 8 am – 6 pm. At other ti mes, keep your keycard with 
you (update: due to COVID -19 restrictions, the doors are closed until further notice). 
 
Anne Simola and Maaria Puupponen (contacts below “ RPU secretaries”) can help to fill out the keycard and 
any possible mechanical key application.

closed until further notice). 
 
Anne Simola and Maaria Puupponen (contacts below “ RPU secretaries”) can help to fill out the keycard and 
any possible mechanical key application. After that Anne/Maaria has processed and signed the application, 
they will tell you to pick it up from Biomedicum info. With your ID and the a pplication you can go to the HUS 
Kiinteistöt /Security Services (right outside Biomedicum, small gray building) to have your pi cture taken 
(opening hours: on Mon -Thu from 9 -12 am and 1 -3 pm and on Fridays from 9 -12 am) . When the keycard is 
ready, you will receive an e -mail to pick it up with your ID from Biomedicum K ey service (2nd floor , room 
A201 : opening hours Mondays, Wednesdays and Fridays fro m 1-2.30 pm) 
Access card validity expired? To renew your access to Biomedicum by sending your keycard number (b ehind 
the card) and your contract expiry date to Anne or Maaria. Also let them know your group and add your PI as 
cc in that email. Maximum validity is two years. 
 
Safety: 
ONCOSYS 3rd floor first aid kit and a fire blanket is located on the wall betwe en B316a and B317a rooms. A 
fire extinguisher is located in the common space B321 and in the kitchen outside the ONCOSYS space. 
- The emergency shower is in the common space B321. 
- Eye wash bottles can be found in the room B 314b (outside our space). We have requested our own eye

ide the ONCOSYS space. 
- The emergency shower is in the common space B321. 
- Eye wash bottles can be found in the room B 314b (outside our space). We have requested our own eye 
wash bottles to be placed next to the emergency shower (20.7). 
- Biomedicum defibrillator can be found in next to the info point in the entrance of the building. 
- Biomedicum’s emergency plan (in English) can be found in the Orientation -folder in the coffee corner 
(room B321). The emergency plan can be read online in Biomedicum intra : 
https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Emergency -Plan.aspx 
- University of Helsinki safety issues (incl. occupation al safety and security etc.) in Flamma: 
https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/turva llisuus

Affiliation and personnel issues : 
Affiliation: 
Research Program in Systems Oncology, Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki 
In Finnish: Systeemisen onkologian tutkimusohjelma, Lääketieteellinen tiedekunta, Helsingin yliopisto 
Check your aff iliation with the ONCOSYS coordinator before submitting an article! 
Research Program in Systems Oncology (ONCOSYS) is a part of the Research Programs Unit (RPU) in the 
Faculty of Medicine, University of Helsinki. Your lab can also have another affiliation (Medicum, Clinicum). 
Depending on the source of your salary or grant, contact personnel of that unit for all personnel issues . 
 
Service team for RPU: 
https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/laaketieteellinen -tiedekunta/palvelutiimi -tutkimusohjelmayksikko 
 
Biomedicum Address 
Visiting address: Biomedicum Helsinki 1, Haartmaninkatu 8, 00290 Helsinki 
Mailing address: Your name, Lab, P.O.Box 63, FI-00014 Helsingin yliopisto

(Check from Flamma https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/talous -ja-hankinnat/yliopiston -laskutusosoite -
pankkitiedot -ja-alv-tunnukset )

VAT number: FI03134717 (international suppliers)

Reporting any damage or problems in the building: (e.g. lights, heating/air -conditioning, clogged pipes) 
- Directions , contact numbers and web form : https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Real -Estate -
Services.aspx Unfortunately the web form is only in Finnish. If you cannot report the problem yours elf, ask 
someone else to do it immediately. To the "Osastot" -section one should always write "9999 Muut 
asiakkaat." It usually takes a few weeks for the issue to be solved. 
- HelpDesk, tel. 471 74800 / 24 h, should be used only in emergency situations, s uch as water and gas leaks, 
gas exchanges etc. 
- Questions? Mikko Leinonen ( mikko.leinonen@hus.fi ) note, he does not have a Helsinki.fi –address, that will 
go to some other person with the same name.

Sick days, holidays (SoleTM, SAP HR) 
(In addition to notifying your PI about sick days and holidays): 
Each university employee with salary : Add your holidays and sick days to Sole TM at the latest on the first 
day of next month. F or example, you were on h oliday 3. -7.2. and sick leave 15. -16.2. You should also 
remember to confirm your working hours in Sole TM. You can do that by logging in 
(http://soletm.it.helsinki.fi ) → Choose “Monthly sums and confirmation” → Click on the magnifying glass of 
the month → Check that your holidays and sick days are marked → Click “Return” → Click “Confirm” and 
then you can log out. 
If you do not add your holidays or sick days to SoleTM , your group will lose money, since the holidays and 
sick days are paid from another source. 
In addition, everyone with salary has to fill in the sick days to SAP HR https://saphr.it.helsinki.fi/irj/portal . 
If you are not a researcher, but support staff, you will also need to apply your holidays to SAP HR .

Page 4 of 8 
 
Those working on a grant do not need to fill in Sole or SAP HR. Those receiving salary from HUS should refer 
to HUS personnel for their regulations. 
 
More on holidays 
Researchers/PhD -students (with salary from the university ) need to discuss with their PI regarding the 
vacation times, but don't have to put them i n SAP HR. However, remember to send the vacation dates also to 
your lab manager/technician . The holiday period for research personnel is from 1.8. to 31.7. The number 
days off equivalent to holidays in that period is approximately 30 days (research personn el follows the 1600 
working hours per year schedule). 
Occupational health: 
Everyone with salary can use the occupational health services: 
https://flamma.helsinki. fi/en/group/henkilostoasiat/occupational -health -care 
Remember to ask if your health issue is covered by the University of if you have to pay it partly by yourself. 
To book an appointment (or to inquire about the coverage) to the occupational heal th physician or nurse call 
010 414 0777 . More info about booki ng an appointment: 
https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/henkilostoasiat/ajanvaraus -tyoterveyshuoltoon 
 
How to purchase a computer/phone 
Flamma: https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/it -ja-puhelin/how -to-purchase -a-computer

/group/henkilostoasiat/ajanvaraus -tyoterveyshuoltoon 
 
How to purchase a computer/phone 
Flamma: https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/it -ja-puhelin/how -to-purchase -a-computer 
You can also ask ONCOSYS IT specialist Pekka Hintsanen for advice: pekka.hintsanen@helsinki.fi 
 
How to purchase IT -supplies : 
Flamma https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/it -ja-puhelin/nain -hankit -it-tarvikkeita 
You can also ask ONCOSYS IT specialist Pekka Hintsanen for advice: pekka.hintsanen@helsinki.fi 
 
Printing, copying and scanning , internet access 
If you have been granted the printing rights, you can print by selecting any printer named “Smartcard” and 
after registering once your keycard to any Smartcard print er (room B320 a) you will be able to print, copy and 
scan with any of the University’s Smartcard printers. If you are unsure about your printing rights you can 
enquire about it from admin -meilahti@helsinki.f i 
Registering your keycard: https://helpdesk.it.helsinki.fi/en/instructions/computer -and-
printing/printing/printing -canon -multi -function -printers 
Reporting printer problems: Report to huoltokutsut@canon.fi (add printer serial number : LYE19423 and 
room : B320A ). 
Intern et access in Biomedicum: https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Information -Technology, -
Data -Commu nications -and-Computer -Server -Room -Facilities.aspx 
 
Travel

ern et access in Biomedicum: https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Information -Technology, -
Data -Commu nications -and-Computer -Server -Room -Facilities.aspx 
 
Travel 
Flamma instructions: https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/matkustaminen 
Those receiving a salary has to submit a travel request before the travel and a travel expense report after the 
trip in SAP Finance https://sapwww.it.helsinki.fi/irj/portal . 
If you are getting a grant, you sho uld ask the coordinator for the travel request document . Your PI should 
sign the document before sending it to your group’s project accountant. Those that receive a grant should 
also fill out a form for reimbursement of travel costs after getting back .

Page 5 of 8 
 
Get your WBS element from your lab manager or PI, EVERY t ime you travel . All travel requests and expense 
reports require a copy of any conference programs that you attend. Also, remember to ask for a travel 
insurance card before your trip from the coordinator. Save all the original receipts, boarding passes and 
official programs from any events that you attend during your trip. A good idea is to take photos of the 
receipts in case you lose the originals. 
 
Flights : Should be bought trough CWT : Instructions for ordering tickets in Flamma 
https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/matkustaminen/matkan -varaaminen 
Hotels usually charge at signing out, so it is best to pay that with your own credit card and get the 
reimbursement of the costs in a travel expense report when you get back 
(https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/matkustaminen/matkalasku ). Remember to save your receipts for the 
reimbursement . 
 
Rooms av ailable for a quiet surrounding 
If you need a quiet working space, you can use the shared rooms A203a+b and A204b (2nd floor A -wing). In 
general, the tables cannot be booked and you need to empty them for the night. When you go for a break, 
you can lock y our things in the lockers that are found in the rooms. There are a few tables that have been 
booked with special permission, the rest are free for everyone to use.

you can lock y our things in the lockers that are found in the rooms. There are a few tables that have been 
booked with special permission, the rest are free for everyone to use. The door to the A -wing spaces is 
normally open between 8 -16.00. If you need to access the fa cilities later, you need to have RPU access rights 
(Biomedicum ID -card or a flexim -key). Currently due to COVID restrictions, the doors are kept locked. 
Rooms available for a meeting (booking is free of charge) 
Biomedicum meeting rooms available: Meeting r oom Kuutti (B328a1) can be booked with the Outlook 
calendar. Instructions can be found in Helpdesk webpages or by contacting the coordinator. In addition 
Pharmacology (B201a) and Anatomy (B101) meeting rooms can be book ed by Anne Simola 
(anne.simola@helsin ki.fi). 
The Medical campus library Terkko has quiet space to work in available for anyone to use (not open for the 
summer) . It’s also possible to book rooms from Terkko: https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/tilat/opetus -ja-
kokoustilojen -varaaminen 
 
Booking any other meeting/seminar rooms or le cture halls in Biomedicum1 or 2 (booking is charge d) 
Check the rooms and their availabilities here: https://tilavaraus.helsinki.fi or only for Biomedicum 1 here 
https://tilavaraus.helsinki.fi/fi/meilahti/biomedicum -haartmaninkatu -8 then ask the ONCOSYS coordinator to 
book it for you.

tilavaraus.helsinki.fi or only for Biomedicum 1 here 
https://tilavaraus.helsinki.fi/fi/meilahti/biomedicum -haartmaninkatu -8 then ask the ONCOSYS coordinator to 
book it for you. Specify the amount of people you are expecting to attend your meeting and what equipment 
is needed. 
 
Wet -lab issues 
Your lab technici an or –manager should know about the following issues: 
Instrument maintenance, l ab equipment – glassware etc ., research e quipment register , registry for 
chemicals, lab coats. 
Problems with lab equi pment? Biomedicum lab engineer s might be able to help : ltdk-
labengineers@helsinki.fi 
 
Unisport 
Instructions on Unisport services: https://flamma.helsinki.fi/en/group/henkilostoasiat/unisport -sports -
services 
- Unisport offers a free 15 -min CareFree shoulders –exercise session every Fri at 12.00 -12.15 in Biomedicum 
1, 5th floor lobby : https://unisport.fi/en/sports/body -control -and-mobility/carefree -shoulders

Page 6 of 8 
 
- UniSport offers Break Pro Break exercise progr am for everyone at the university. Break Pr o application can 
be downloaded to your work computer from the software center. Instructions on Helpdesk’s page 
https://helpdesk.it.helsinki.fi/en/instructions/computer -and-printing/software/software -center 
 
Biomedicum building info , lockers and deliveries : 
- The building has lockers and showers in the P -floor (u se your own keycard to get in), more info in the intra 
pages https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Lockers.aspx . There are 37 lockers in the ladies' 
dressing room and 37 lockers in the m en's dressing room. The receptionists are in charge of dist ributing free 
lockers and keys. There are a few lockers available for occasional users so that they may leave their 
belongings safely in a locked closet when showering. 
- You can pick up any delive ries at the info or in the delivery pick -up point in the loading dock . The security 
personnel at the info desk can help you with deliveries . 
- There is also a trolley to borrow at the info , if you need it. Remember to return the trolley immediately. 
- More about deliveries and courier services can be found in the intra pages 
https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Mail, -Courier -Mail, -Cargo -Delivery.aspx 
 
Mailing rooms

about deliveries and courier services can be found in the intra pages 
https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Mail, -Courier -Mail, -Cargo -Delivery.aspx 
 
Mailing rooms 
Mailing rooms are located in the 3rd floor near the central elevators. Each lab has an own mailing box. You 
can also find the recycling collection for cardboard boxes there. Packaging material for DHL etc. can be found 
there. A recycling bin for batteries should also be found in the room. 
 
Waste management (e.g. styrox /thermocol , cardboard, batteries, bio, confidential papers ) 
Biomedicum waste management instructions: https://biomedicumfi.sharepoint.com/SitePages/Waste -
management.aspx 
- Orange bin: everything that burns and that cannot be recycled in any other recycle bins 
- Black bag: everything that is not recyclable and not hazardous waste 
- Paper bin: green bins next to the printer in B321. 
- Organic waste (edible stuff, not samples): green organic waste bag in the common room or in the kitchen 
- Cardboard recycling: in the mailing room 
- Styrox (Thermocol) material needs to be taken to the recycling station outside the loading dock of 
Biomedicum 1. 
- Any confidential paper s should be taken to a separate locked bin for that purpose. They can be found in the 
mailing room or in the central waste management room in the P -floor.

ny confidential paper s should be taken to a separate locked bin for that purpose. They can be found in the 
mailing room or in the central waste management room in the P -floor. 
- P-floor waste management: biological -, glass -, hazardous - and electron ic waste etc. 
- More instructions in the extensive waste management link above 
 
ONCOSYS common issues: 
 
ONCOSYS mailing list: oncosys -all@helsinki.fi 
The RPU has an own mailing list: rpu-all@helsinki.fi 
- If you have recently started working in any of the ONCOSYS groups, please check with the ONCOSYS 
coordinator that you are on your group’s mailing list, hence also on the oncosys -all and rpu -all –lists. 
- If you need to share your news to the whole Biomedicum, send your e -mail to Emili a Carlsson , 
emilia.carlsson@biomedicum.fi, 040 0716 149 (communications coordinator) and ask her to share it with the 
necessary audience.

Page 7 of 8 
 
SLACK : ONCOSYS has our own Slack community for internal communication: ask the ONCOSYS coordinator 
for the invitation link. Remember to check out the different channels every now and then. You can also 
create your own private or public (to ONCO SYS) channels. 
Graph Pad Prism : 
All Graph Pad Prism orders in ONCOSYS will be made through a common e -form. Before making an order, 
please consult with your PI. The cost is around 50 USD/li cense. 
- Link to the e -form: https://elomake.helsinki.fi/lomakkeet/107956/lomakkeet.html (valid ad. Sept. 25th) 
- The orders will be placed once a month, approximately on the 22nd of each month. Once the orders have 
been made, the users will receive an email with instructions on h ow to install the program etc. 
- Pleas e remember, that you SHOULD NOT use the activation link sent to another person (or an old activation 
link if you’ve had a license from the collective order before), even though this is technically possible. 
- Send an email to the program coordinat or (karen .ahlnas@helsinki.fi ) if you need to rene w your li cense . 
ONCOSYS Progress Report 
Usually about every s econd Friday at 15.00 -16.00. Program and more info for the speakers and attendees 
can be found here: http://tiny.cc/oncosysprogressreport 
Happy hours (canceled until further notice du e to COVID -restri ctions)

am and more info for the speakers and attendees 
can be found here: http://tiny.cc/oncosysprogressreport 
Happy hours (canceled until further notice du e to COVID -restri ctions) 
If nothing else is notified, we have a Happy hour ea ch Friday at 16.00 -17.00 in the common room B321. 
Please write your name in the attendance list upon arriving to the Happy hour. Also, remember to put any 
empty cans in the cardboard box for recycling. 
 
ONCOSYS common space room B321 
Coffee machine 
– Ever yone are in charge of notifying the ONCOSYS coordinator immediately in case of malfunction that is 
not fixed right away by anyone else. If the coordinator is unavailable, also the personnel at CoffeeWorks can 
help: Hans Lagerström hans.lagerstrom@coffeeworks.fi , +358 50 4923384. 
- Everyone should add coffee beans and milk to the mini -fridge if they ran out. DO NOT USE ANY OTHER MILK 
than cow’s milk with the machine, because it wil l lead to a blockage in the milk system. 
- The user manual and trouble shooting guide are found next to the coffee machine or in the wall behind the 
machine. 
- Milk system cleaning has to be done every day at the earliest at 16.00, but at the latest by th e last person in 
the ONCOSYS space. You should not prepare milk coffees after that the milk system cleaning has been done, 
for hygienic reasons and to avoid blocking the milk system of the machine.

he ONCOSYS space. You should not prepare milk coffees after that the milk system cleaning has been done, 
for hygienic reasons and to avoid blocking the milk system of the machine. You can see when the cleaning 
has been done in the sheet n ext to the machine. Also the milk tube will appear clear (has water inside) when 
the cleaning has been done. Do not prepare milk coffees outside office hours if you cannot make the milk 
system cleaning afterwards. 
- Minimum one to two people per lab should be able to perform milk cleaning. Ask the coordinator for help 
to learn how to do the milk system cleaning. 
- The coffee machine also needs a weekly a cleaning (~20 min) and decalcification about once every month 
(~40 min). At the moment there is only a few people within ONCOSYS who knows how to do this. Ask the 
coordinator for assistance if you would like to help out. 
- The coordinator will order coffee, tea, milk, oatmilk and sweeteners. Inform if something is running out. 
 
We have common glass cups that are meant main ly for visitors, please bring your own cup to work . The 
disposable cups are bought for take away -purposes . If you use t he common cups, please remember to wash 
your cup by the end of the day and return it when dry to the cupboard. We are also sharing com mon bowls 
and cutlery which are to be returned clean and dry if you borrow them for your lab events.

Page 8 of 8 
 
Common fridge/freezer : Mark all the items with a name, and opening date! Any items that are found to be 
very old and going bad will be thrown away. Keep the fridge c lean. 
 
Keep the common space clean! Try not to stain the new sofa or the blanket. Wipe any possible stains out 
immediately. 
3rd floor kitchen 
The kitchen is shared with the whole 3rd floor B -wing. Please keep it clean and also mark any of your personal 
items with a name and date if you put it in the fridge. Remove your own old food from the fridge before they 
go moldy , please . ONCOSYS has common dishwashing liquid that can be found in the cupboard in the left 
upper corner of the kitchen. Two common microwaves are available, please keep it also clean.
